title: Wall Street is getting trampled by an AI sell-off. South Korean market plunges 10%
summary: Wall Street is getting trampled by an AI sell-off. South Korean market plunges 10%
extracted_text: Nasdaq dropped 2.21% (worst day in ~2 weeks). S&P 500 fell 1.44%. Dow slipped just 0.1%. Kospi tumbled 10% on Tuesday, triggering a circuit breaker and 20-minute cooling-off period. SK Hynix and Samsung plummeted more than 12% each. Nvidia (NVDA): $200.04 (-8.61, -4.13%). Micron (MU): -13%, still +269% YTD. Oracle (ORCL): -5.5%, down ~27% this month. Marvell (MRVL): -9%. Google (GOOG): < -1%, recovered after Mon -5%. SpaceX (SPCX): +~1%. Fear & Greed Index: 27 — Fear. James Reilly, Capital Economics: 'These big moves are part of a growing trend of rising volatility in tech stocks generally. This volatility is evidence of excessive froth and calls into question the sustainability of this rally.' On Wednesday morning, Kospi bounced back 3%, Samsung surged 7%.

title: Global Economy Briefing — June 24, 2026
extracted_text: Market snapshot: S&P 500 7,365.45 (-1.44%), Nasdaq 25,588.32 (-2.21%), Dow 51,665.18 (-0.09%), Russell 2,975.55 (-0.96%). 30Y/10Y Treasury: 5.03%/4.50%. 2Y: 4.19% +0.12% (highest since Feb 2025). WTI Crude: $72.58 (-0.86%), Brent: $76.34 (-0.96%), Gold: $4,107 (-0.56%), Copper: $6.15 (+0.11%), Bitcoin: $62,651 (-0.03%). US Manufacturing PMI 55.7 (vs 54.6 expected). Key insight: 'The pattern was one of rotation under stress: not investors fleeing the market, but reshuffling away from the crowded, expensive corners of it.' About half of all US shares rose. PCE inflation due Thu June 25, new home sales Wed June 25, GDP Thu June 26.

title: Treasury Yield Curve Today: June 24, 2026 — Rates & Spread Monitor
summary: Treasury Yield Curve Today: June 24, 2026 — Rates & Spread Monitor
extracted_text: 3M: 3.85% (+2bp), 1Y: 4.04% (+4bp), 2Y: 4.24% (+5bp), 5Y: 4.29% (+6bp), 7Y: 4.39% (+5bp), 10Y: 4.51% (+5bp), 20Y: 4.97% (+6bp), 30Y: 4.95% (+5bp). Key spreads: 10Y-2Y +0.27pp, 10Y-3M +0.66pp. Breakeven: 5Y 2.28%, 10Y 2.23%. Quote: 'Treasury yields climbed across the curve in the June 22 session as markets continued to digest the Federal Reserve's more hawkish June projections.' 'The median dot for end-2026 rose to 3.8% from 3.4% in March, signaling officials now lean toward at least one more rate increase this year.' 'The 2026 inflation outlook was lifted to 3.6% headline and 3.3% core.'

title: Asian stocks are mixed after Big Tech sell-off
summary: Asian stocks are mixed after Big Tech sell-off
extracted_text: James Reilly, Capital Economics: 'This is an illustration of rising volatility in these stocks. This is particularly true in Korea where domestic retail buyers are taking on an increasing role.' ING strategists: 'Price movements suggest the market expects a fairly rapid recovery in Persian Gulf oil supplies.' Kospi +3.3% to 8,471.02, Samsung +9.8% (after -12.3%), SK Hynix +1%. Nikkei -0.9% to 69,174.97 (after -3.6%). Taiwan -2.2%. PCE report due Thursday — Fed's preferred inflation gauge. Bond yields stayed higher.

title: KOSPI Crash 2026: Korea's AI Chip Reckoning Explained
summary: KOSPI Crash 2026: Korea's AI Chip Reckoning Explained
extracted_text: KOSPI close: 8,203.84 (-910.71 pts, -10.0%). Circuit breaker triggered at 2:33 PM local time after 8% drop. Samsung -12.31% to 310,000 KRW. SK Hynix -12.47% to 2,555,000 KRW. Tokyo Electron and Advantest aggressively sold by international funds. Key insight: 'The real question after a semiconductor selloff is not Is AI over? It is How much future AI growth was already priced into the stocks?' Five risk layers: demand risk, margin risk, capacity risk, customer concentration, market positioning.

title: US Dollar Short-term Outlook: USD Breakout Targets Next Major Resistance
summary: US Dollar Short-term Outlook: USD Breakout Targets Next Major Resistance
extracted_text: DXY broke to fresh yearly high. USD/JPY 161.69 (up from 161.55). EUR/USD $1.1354 (down from $1.1382). DXY 101.38 on June 23, up 0.36% from prior session. DXY strengthened 2.16% over past month, 3.60% over last 12 months. Driven by hawkish FOMC projections signaling at least one more rate hike in 2026.

title: CBOE Volatility Index: VIX
extracted_text: VIX Spot Price: $19.49 (as of June 23, 2026). Change: 12.79% (+2.21). Most Active Calls: Strike 20.00, Expiry 7/22/2026. Most Active Puts: Strike 19.00, Expiry 7/1/2026. FRED data shows VIX at 17.28 on June 22 — the 2.21 point spike reflects the KOSPI-led chip selloff.
